message.content:
Read C:\Users\Administrator\.claude\CLAUDE.md and create an optimized version:

1. Move all code examples (csharp blocks, folder structure, markdown examples) to a NEW reference file: C:\Users\Administrator\.claude\references\project-structure-guide.md
2. In CLAUDE.md, replace code examples with a one-line reference pointer: "See references/project-structure-guide.md for examples"
3. Keep ALL rules/requirements in CLAUDE.md but make them more concise - one line per rule where possible
4. Target: CLAUDE.md under 60 lines

Write both files. Keep everything in English.

content.text: Let me start by reading the current CLAUDE.md file.

content.content:
1→# Global Rules
     2→
     3→## Response
     4→- Respond in Korean, code comments in **English**. Be concise, no flattery/chatter
     5→- Propose approach before modifying code, ask first if spec is unclear
     6→
     7→## Git
     8→- Email: ryudae33@gmail.com / GitHub: ryudae33 (run `gh auth switch --user ryudae33` before work)
     9→- Org: ftech-projects (repos default under `ftech-projects/`)
    10→- **After git push, always run `gdrive-sync push`** — backs up gitignored docs/DB files to Google Drive `Sourcr ryudae/repo-name/`
    11→- **Build publish artifacts**: not tracked by git. Manual `gdrive-sync push` before deploy is project-specific (build-runner does not auto-sync)
    12→- **After git clone, always run `gdrive-sync pull`** — restores gitignored docs/DB files from Google Drive
    13→- **Commit messages must include clear change summary**: format `<Project>: <summary>`, use bullet points in body for multiple changes
    14→
    15→## Coding
    16→- PascalCase (classes/methods), camelCase (variables), ALL_CAPS (constants)
    17→- Ask first if HW/protocol info is insufficient
    18→- **Tech suggestions**: When writing code related to industrial automation, robotics, serial/TCP communication, or sensor integration, proactively suggest newer/better libraries, tools, or approaches if available (e.g., newer NuGet packages, GitHub trending repos, better protocols). Focus areas: .NET, WinForms/WPF, embedded, PLC, cobot, servo/motor control, vision, IoT
    19→
    20→## Project Structure (LLM-Optimized)
    21→ALL projects (new and existing) MUST follow this structure. When modifying legacy projects, progressively refactor toward this structure — split large files, extract features, separate config.
    22→
    23→### File Rules
    24→- **Max 200 lines per file** — split if exceeding. LLM reads entire file per edit; large files waste tokens
    25→- **Self-descriptive naming** — `MoveServoToPosition(int servoIndex, double targetMm)` not `DoWork(int m, double v)`
    26→- **No magic numbers in code** — all constants in config or dedicated constants class
    27→
    28→### Feature-Based Folder Structure (not layered MVC)
    29→```
    30→src/
    31→  Features/
    32→    Measurement/
    33→      MeasurementService.cs    # business logic
    34→      MeasurementConfig.cs     # settings/constants
    35→      MeasurementForm.cs       # UI (thin, delegates to Service)
    36→    ServoControl/
    37→      ServoService.cs
    38→      ServoConfig.cs
    39→      ServoForm.cs
    40→  Shared/
    41→    Communication/             # serial/TCP/Modbus helpers
    42→    Database/                  # DB access helpers
    43→    Models/                    # shared DTOs/enums
    44→  Program.cs                   # entry point + global exception handler
    45→  config.json                  # all runtime settings (ports, speeds, thresholds)
    46→```
    47→- Each feature folder is self-contained: UI + logic + config
    48→- LLM finds target file by feature name without Glob/Grep → saves search tokens
    49→
    50→### Config Separation
    51→- Runtime settings (ports, speeds, thresholds) → `config.json` or `appsettings.json`
    52→- Changing a value = editing JSON, not parsing 1000-line code file
    53→- Use strongly-typed config classes to deserialize
    54→
    55→### State Machine as Data
    56→- Define steps/sequences as enum + Dictionary or data structure, not giant switch-case
    57→- Step additions/changes become data edits, not logic refactoring
    58→```csharp
    59→enum Step { Init, Move, Measure, Evaluate }
    60→Dictionary<Step, StepConfig> Steps = new() {
    61→    [Step.Init] = new("초기화", ServoAction.Home, nextStep: Step.Move),
    62→    [Step.Move] = new("이동", ServoAction.MoveToPos, nextStep: Step.Measure),
    63→};
    64→```
    65→
    66→### Interface-Based Devices
    67→- All devices implement common interface → LLM can add new device without reading existing implementations
    68→```csharp
    69→public interface IDevice { void Connect(); void Disconnect(); double Read(); }
    70→```
    71→
    72→### Project CLAUDE.md File Map
    73→- Every new project MUST have a `CLAUDE.md` in project root with a file map section:
    74→```markdown
    75→## File Map
    76→- Features/Measurement/ — load cell/LVDT/gauge measurement
    77→- Features/ServoControl/ — servo control and positioning
    78→- Shared/Communication/ — serial/TCP communication helpers
    79→```
    80→- This eliminates exploratory file searches → biggest token saver
    81→
    82→## New Project Baseline
    83→- **Global exception handler**: `ThreadException` + `UnhandledException` → crash.log (time/source/message/stack). Thread/timer try-catch mandatory
    84→- **ScottPlot Korean font**: `ScottPlot.Fonts.Default = "Malgun Gothic";` set globally in OnStartup
    85→
    86→## Orchestration
    87→- Delegate complex tasks (analysis/build/DB/comm/docs) to specialized subagents
    88→- Criteria: use Task tool for single-domain focused work or parallelizable tasks
    89→- Key agents: project-analyzer, build-runner, db-explorer, log-analyzer, hw-connector, doc-reader, web-searcher, git-manager
    90→- Handle simple questions/single file edits directly (no agent abuse)
    91→
    92→## Token & Context Optimization
    93→- **All new features, structural changes, and plugin/MCP additions must prioritize token savings and context optimization**
    94→- Avoid redundant plugins/MCP servers that duplicate existing CLI tool capabilities (e.g., `github` plugin when `gh` CLI already works)
    95→- MCP tool schemas load into context every message — only install plugins that provide genuinely new capabilities
    96→- Prefer Bash CLI tools over MCP plugins when functionality overlaps
    97→- Keep system prompt lean: minimize skills, agents, and tools loaded per session
    98→
    99→## Config Files Language
   100→- All config/memory/reference files (`~/.claude/` including CLAUDE.md, commands/, agents/, references/, memory/) must be written in **English** to minimize token consumption
   101→
   102→## Work Log
   103→- Check project CLAUDE.md on start, record time + details on completion (`powershell Get-Date`, no UTC)
   104→
   105→## Tool References
   106→CLI/DB/comm/sync tool details in `~/.claude/references/`:
   107→- `tools-cli.md` — 7-Zip, Everything, rclone, LibreOffice, SumatraPDF, WSL
   108→- `tools-db.md` — SQL Server(sqlcmd), SQLite(sqlite3), Access(OleDb)
   109→- `tools-comm.md` — Serial(miniterm/plink), TCP(ncat), winsocat, tshark
   110→- `tools-sync.md` — gdrive-sync, claude-config-sync usage
   111→- `tools-screenshot.md` — PowerShell screenshot capture code
   112→

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. Let me create both files.

input.content:
# Project Structure Guide

## Feature-Based Folder Structure
```
src/
  Features/
    Measurement/
      MeasurementService.cs    # business logic
      MeasurementConfig.cs     # settings/constants
      MeasurementForm.cs       # UI (thin, delegates to Service)
    ServoControl/
      ServoService.cs
      ServoConfig.cs
      ServoForm.cs
  Shared/
    Communication/             # serial/TCP/Modbus helpers
    Database/                  # DB access helpers
    Models/                    # shared DTOs/enums
  Program.cs                   # entry point + global exception handler
  config.json                  # all runtime settings (ports, speeds, thresholds)
```

## State Machine as Data
Define steps/sequences as enum + Dictionary, not giant switch-case:
```csharp
enum Step { Init, Move, Measure, Evaluate }
Dictionary<Step, StepConfig> Steps = new() {
    [Step.Init] = new("초기화", ServoAction.Home, nextStep: Step.Move),
    [Step.Move] = new("이동", ServoAction.MoveToPos, nextStep: Step.Measure),
};
```

## Interface-Based Devices
All devices implement common interface for easy extension:
```csharp
public interface IDevice { void Connect(); void Disconnect(); double Read(); }
```

## Project CLAUDE.md File Map Example
Every project root CLAUDE.md should include:
```markdown
## File Map
- Features/Measurement/ — load cell/LVDT/gauge measurement
- Features/ServoControl/ — servo control and positioning
- Shared/Communication/ — serial/TCP communication helpers
```
This eliminates exploratory file searches — biggest token saver.

# Global Rules

## Response
- Respond in Korean, code comments in **English**. Be concise, no flattery/chatter
- Propose approach before modifying code, ask first if spec is unclear

## Git
- Email: ryudae33@gmail.com / GitHub: ryudae33 (`gh auth switch --user ryudae33` before work)
- Org: ftech-projects (repos default under `ftech-projects/`)
- After git push → always run `gdrive-sync push` (backs up gitignored docs/DB to Google Drive)
- After git clone → always run `gdrive-sync pull` (restores gitignored docs/DB)
- Build publish artifacts: not git-tracked, manual `gdrive-sync push` before deploy
- Commit format: `<Project>: <summary>`, bullet points in body for multiple changes

## Coding
- PascalCase (classes/methods), camelCase (variables), ALL_CAPS (constants)
- Ask first if HW/protocol info is insufficient
- Proactively suggest newer libraries/tools for industrial automation, robotics, serial/TCP, sensors (.NET, WinForms/WPF, embedded, PLC, cobot, servo, vision, IoT)

## Project Structure
- ALL projects MUST follow feature-based folder structure. Legacy projects: progressively refactor
- Max 200 lines per file — split if exceeding (LLM reads entire file per edit)
- Self-descriptive naming — no magic numbers in code, all constants in config/constants class
- Each feature folder is self-contained: UI + logic + config
- Runtime settings → `config.json`/`appsettings.json` with strongly-typed config classes
- State machines as data (enum + Dictionary), not giant switch-case
- All devices implement common interface (`IDevice`) for easy extension
- Every new project MUST have `CLAUDE.md` with file map section
- See `references/project-structure-guide.md` for examples

## New Project Baseline
- Global exception handler: `ThreadException` + `UnhandledException` → crash.log. Thread/timer try-catch mandatory
- ScottPlot Korean font: `ScottPlot.Fonts.Default = "Malgun Gothic";` in OnStartup

## Orchestration
- Delegate complex tasks to subagents (project-analyzer, build-runner, db-explorer, log-analyzer, hw-connector, doc-reader, web-searcher, git-manager)
- Use Task tool for single-domain focused work or parallelizable tasks
- Handle simple questions/single file edits directly (no agent abuse)

## Token & Context Optimization
- All new features/structural changes/MCP additions must prioritize token savings
- No redundant MCP plugins that duplicate CLI tools (e.g., `github` plugin vs `gh` CLI)
- Prefer Bash CLI tools over MCP plugins when functionality overlaps
- Keep system prompt lean: minimize skills, agents, tools loaded per session

## Config Files Language
- All files in `~/.claude/` (CLAUDE.md, commands/, agents/, references/, memory/) must be in **English**

## Work Log
- Check project CLAUDE.md on start, record time + details on completion (`powershell Get-Date`, no UTC)

## Tool References
CLI/DB/comm/sync tool details in `~/.claude/references/`:
- `tools-cli.md` — 7-Zip, Everything, rclone, LibreOffice, SumatraPDF, WSL
- `tools-db.md` — SQL Server(sqlcmd), SQLite(sqlite3), Access(OleDb)
- `tools-comm.md` — Serial(miniterm/plink), TCP(ncat), winsocat, tshark
- `tools-sync.md` — gdrive-sync, claude-config-sync usage
- `tools-screenshot.md` — PowerShell screenshot capture code
- `project-structure-guide.md` — folder structure, state machine, interface, file map examples

content.content: The file C:\Users\Administrator\.claude\CLAUDE.md has been updated successfully.

2. **`C:\Users\Administrator\.claude\references\project-structure-guide.md`** (신규) — 코드 예시 4개 모두 포함

   - State machine as data (enum + Dictionary)
   - Interface-based devices (`IDevice`)

모든 규칙/요구사항은 CLAUDE.md에 그대로 유지되어 있고, 예시만 참조 파일로 분리했습니다.
